title: cassia stenocarpa var. stenocarpoides
intro: Cassia stenocarpa var. stenocarpoides, also known as the Yellow Cassia or Golden Shower Tree, is a plant species native to tropical Australia and New Guinea. It is a member of the Fabaceae family and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat digestive issues, fever, and skin problems. The plant's leaves, bark, and seeds are used in herbal preparations to treat a range of health issues, including inflammation, wounds, and respiratory problems. In traditional medicine, Cassia stenocarpa var. stenocarpoides is used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that are said to have antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties.
